query: Mexico mobile line registration deadline June 30 2026 extension postponement CRT latest news June 2026
answer:
# Mexico Mobile Line Registration Deadline — Research Report (June 2, 2026)

## Executive Summary

Mexico has a **June 30, 2026 deadline** for all mobile phone users to register their lines by linking them to their **CURP** (national population registry code). The **CRT** (Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones) has **explicitly and repeatedly ruled out any extension or postponement**. As of June 1–2, 2026, approximately **92 million lines remain unregistered** out of ~161.7 million total — meaning only ~43% compliance with fewer than 28 days remaining.

---

## 1. Background: What Is the Registration Requirement?

This is **not** the old PANAUT (Padrón Nacional de Usuarios de Telefonía Móvil), which was declared **unconstitutional** by Mexico's Supreme Court (SCJN) in **April 2022** due to biometric data privacy concerns.

The **current** framework was enacted on **January 9, 2026** under the **CRT** (successor to the IFT as Mexico's telecom regulator). Key features:
- All mobile users must link their phone number to their **CURP**
- Applies to **all line types**: prepaid, postpaid, physical SIM, and eSIM
- Applies to Mexican nationals, foreign residents, and tourists with active Mexican lines
- Accepted ID: INE voter card, Mexican passport; foreigners use valid passport
- Companies register via their **RFC** (Federal Taxpayer Registry)
- **Does NOT require biometric data** (unlike the old PANAUT)
- Registration data is held by telecom operators, NOT a centralized government database

---

## 2. The June 30, 2026 Deadline: Confirmed, No Extension

The deadline is **June 30, 2026**, and the CRT has been emphatic that **no extension (prórroga) will be granted**:

- **Ricardo Castañeda** (CRT Director General of Regulatory Policy) stated on Radio Fórmula: *"Es un plazo legal, está establecido en la ley. No lo tenemos [prórroga] sobre la mesa."* ("It is a legal deadline, established in the law. We do not have [an extension] on the table.")
- **La Jornada (April 1, 2026):** "No habrá prórroga para el registro de líneas telefónicas: CRT"
- **DPL News (April 20, 2026):** The CRT president herself confirmed no extension
- **Mediotiempo (May 13, 2026):** CRT reiterated: no extension
- **Proyecto Puente (May 29, 2026):** "Registro de línea celular no tendrá prórroga"
- **El Heraldo de Saltillo (May 29, 2026):** "La CRT descarta prórroga para registrar línea celular"
- **Maya Comunicación (May 29, 2026):** CRT rules out extension; unlinked lines will be suspended

---

## 3. Registration Progress — Very Low Compliance

| Date | Lines Registered | Total Lines | % Registered |
|------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|
| April 1, 2026 | ~26 million | ~161.7 million | ~16% |
| May 13–22, 2026 | ~48 million | ~161.7 million | ~30% |
| May 27, 2026 | ~51.6 million | ~161.7 million | ~32% |
| June 1–2, 2026 | ~69.7 million | ~161.7 million | ~43% |

- As of **June 2, 2026**: approximately **92 million lines remain unregistered**
- Telecom analyst **Ernesto Piedras** stated publicly: *"Registration of 161.7 million telephone lines is not going to be achieved"*
- Carriers **Telcel and AT&T** have already disconnected ~1 million unregistered mobile lines year-to-date

---

## 4. Consequences for Non-Registration

Lines **not linked to a CURP by June 30, 2026** will be **suspended**:
- **Cannot** make or receive calls
- **Cannot** send SMS messages
- **No** mobile data access
- **Only** remaining function: ability to receive emergency messages (including seismic alert system)
- Suspension is **administrative**, not permanent cancellation
- Service can be **restored** once registration is completed with the operator
- **No direct financial penalties** for users who fail to register

---

## 5. Key Takeaways

1. The **CRT has been unequivocal**: no extension will be granted — the deadline is statutory, not administrative
2. Despite this firmness, **compliance is dangerously low** (~43% with 28 days remaining)
3. The massive gap between registered and total lines (~92 million unregistered) raises practical questions about enforcement
4. Industry analysts consider full registration by the deadline **impossible**
5. Carriers have already begun disconnecting unregistered lines proactively

---

key_facts: Mexico's mobile line registration deadline is June 30, 2026 — users must link their phone number to their CURP (national population registry code).
The CRT (Comisión Reguladora de Telecomunicaciones) has explicitly and repeatedly ruled out any extension (prórroga) of the deadline, stating it is a statutory/legal deadline.
This is NOT the old PANAUT (declared unconstitutional in April 2022). The current framework was enacted January 9, 2026 and does not require biometric data.
As of June 1-2, 2026, approximately 92 million lines remain unregistered out of ~161.7 million total (~43% compliance).
Unregistered lines after June 30 will be suspended (no calls, SMS, or data) but can be restored once registration is completed. Emergency alerts will still function.
Telecom analyst Ernesto Piedras publicly stated that registering all 161.7 million lines by the deadline is impossible.
Carriers Telcel and AT&T have already disconnected approximately 1 million unregistered mobile lines year-to-date.
CRT Director General Ricardo Castañeda stated: 'It is a legal deadline, established in the law. We do not have [an extension] on the table.'
Registration applies to all line types: prepaid, postpaid, physical SIM, and eSIM, for Mexican nationals, foreign residents, and tourists.
No direct financial penalties exist for users who fail to register; the consequence is service suspension.

data_points.metric: Lines already disconnected by carriers (Telcel and AT&T)
